event.summary: Entered validation with a deterministic command, instruction, graph, and device-impact plan.

acceptance.proof: Exact capacity bounds the queue; FIFO values preserve timestamp/value bits; try-push reports Full with unchanged ownership and try-pop reports Empty.
Explicit nonzero wait slice and deadline bound every blocking call; cancellation and close wake blocked callers with typed outcomes and no owned background task.
Exactly one accepted loopback Float32 sample can enter and leave the bounded FIFO with unchanged raw timestamp and value bits.
No retry/recovery policy, smoothing/dejitter, additional sample formats, async runtime, devices, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit the bounded queue, explicit backpressure/cancellation state, loopback composition, tests, and routing coherently before Standard validation; keep lifecycle and push commits intentional.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route bounded queue, backpressure, cancellation, and close behavior while excluding recovery and authority.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run owner and focused operational gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Describe only the bounded caller-owned operational queue surface.
Document queue ownership, state, bounds, cancellation, and wakeup behavior.
Route backpressure, blocking cancellation, close, deadline, and loopback composition checks.
Review bounded data-plane ownership and cancellation; no shared mutation expected.

non_scope: Retry, reconnection, recovery generations, replay, packet changes, additional channel counts or formats, chunks, conversion, smoothing, dejitter, clock acquisition, or periodic scheduling.
Owned worker threads, async runtime, global queue, ambient activation, endpoint selection, performance guarantees, devices, unsafe, FFI, or dependencies.

Official implementation source, copied or translated source, private artifacts, inbound-sample commands, or Manifold admission, registry, session, route, topology, lease, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
document.objective: Add one dependency-free caller-owned bounded FIFO for accepted timestamped single-channel Float32 samples, with explicit nonblocking backpressure, finite blocking waits, cancellation, close/wakeup behavior, and one composition with the accepted loopback sample runtime.
outputs: One bounded caller-owned FIFO whose nonzero capacity is fixed before fallible allocation and whose full outcome returns the unchanged sample.
Finite blocking push/pop calls with explicit wait slice, total deadline, cancellation, close, and wakeup outcomes.
Focused tests for FIFO exact-bit preservation, full/empty backpressure, blocked cancellation, close wakeups, deadlines, and composition with one accepted loopback sample.
